summary:
The correct initial vaccine administration includes DTaP, IPV, and MMR based on current CDC guidelines for unvaccinated children starting a catch-up series at 18 months. All three vaccines target major infectious diseases—diphtheria/tetanus/pertussis/polio/measles/mumps/rubella—that remain significant public health concerns worldwide despite improved prevention strategies.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$

We are only asked to summarize the *agent’s* reasoning — so we must extract just what they said about why their choice was valid clinically.

Only include essential facts directly tied to disease coverage and guideline-based decisions made by an expert clinician evaluating real-world pediatric care needs.

Avoid adding external interpretation beyond what appears explicitly stated within Agent2’s own text.

No mention of tools used unless part of direct medical justification; focus strictly on content derived from retrieval results related to patient management.

Keep exactly two concise sentences focusing solely upon clinical relevance:

- Identify which specific vaccines should be given
- Explain briefly how these choices meet established criteria under normal circumstances without regional exceptions being assumed
